summary: Israel’s air defenses intercepted all missiles launched from Iran, including two ballistic missiles aimed at northern Israel. Iran halted civilian flights at Imam Khomeini Airport and warned of further strikes, while Hezbollah fired rockets toward Israeli border communities. U.S. officials, including President Trump, urged restraint and continued diplomatic engagement.
key_points: Israel’s air force reported intercepting every missile launched from Iran, confirming eleven launches (Source: @VahidOnline/75999).
Two Iranian ballistic missiles targeting northern Israel were successfully intercepted by Israeli air defenses (Source: @manniefabian/58479).
Iran used solid‑fuel Chaybah ballistic missiles from western bases, claiming a range of 1,450 km, weight 4,500 kg, targeting Ramat David (Source: @N12chat/211457).
Israeli minister Itamar Ben‑Gvir called for Tehran to be set ablaze following the missile wave (Source: @VahidOnline/76007).
Iran’s Civil Aviation Authority suspended all flights at Imam Khomeini Airport until further notice (Source: @VahidOnline/76008).
Hezbollah fired rockets toward Israeli border settlements, prompting Israeli alerts in the north (Source: @yediotnews25/130752).
U.S. President Trump said he would call Netanyahu to advise against retaliating against Iran (Source: @pkpoi/142513).

sections.executive_summary: Israel’s air defenses intercepted all Iranian missile launches, including two ballistic missiles aimed at the north (Source: @VahidOnline/75999; @manniefabian/58479). Iran halted civilian flights at Imam Khomeini Airport and warned of further strikes, while Hezbollah fired rockets toward Israeli border communities (Source: @VahidOnline/76008; @yediotnews25/130752). U.S. officials, including President Trump, urged restraint and continued diplomatic engagement (Source: @pkpoi/142513).

points: Iran launched eleven missiles toward Israel, all of which were intercepted by Israeli air defenses, according to the Israeli Air Force (Source: @VahidOnline/75999).
Two ballistic missiles were fired from Iran at northern Israel and were shot down by Israeli air defenses (Source: @manniefabian/58479).
Iran’s missile arsenal included solid‑fuel Chaybah rockets with a 1,450 km range, 4,500 kg weight, launched from western bases toward Ramat David (Source: @N12chat/211457).
Iran’s Civil Aviation Authority ordered a suspension of all flights at Imam Khomeini Airport until further notice amid heightened tensions (Source: @VahidOnline/76008).
Iranian officials warned that any further Israeli aggression in Lebanon or against Iran would be met with a “crushing and tragic” response (Source: @asafroz/49929).
The Israeli Air Force announced it had intercepted every missile launched from Iran, confirming eleven launches (Source: @VahidOnline/75999).
Two Iranian ballistic missiles targeting northern Israel were successfully intercepted by Israeli air defenses (Source: @manniefabian/58479).
Israeli minister Itamar Ben‑Gvir publicly demanded that Tehran be set on fire following the missile attacks (Source: @VahidOnline/76007).
IDF statements indicated readiness for possible rocket fire from Hezbollah in Lebanon and ongoing defensive posture (Source: @manniefabian/58475).
Israeli forces reported a shooting attack in central Israel resulting in one dead and at least five injured (Source: @mamlekate/103627).
Hezbollah fired rockets toward Israeli border settlements, prompting alerts in the north (Source: @yediotnews25/130752).
Israeli forces carried out an operation in the Zahia area of Beirut, after which Hezbollah members expressed shock (Source: @mamlekate/103627).
Iranian commander Ali Abdollahi warned that any escalation in southern Lebanon would trigger a massive retaliatory strike against Israel (Source: @asafroz/49929).
U.S. officials reported that a senior American asked Trump to urge Netanyahu not to respond to the Lebanese missile wave, aiming to preserve ongoing negotiations (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72769).
A blast was reported in western Tehran province at 01:14 local time (Source: @shin_persian/6676).
Explosions were heard shaking Baghdad, with multiple blasts reported (Source: @shin_persian/6677).
Sounds of explosions were also reported in Sulaymaniyah, Iraq (Source: @SharghDaily/164465).
Iranian FM announced telephone contacts with Pakistani, Saudi, French, Qatari, and Iraqi officials regarding regional security (Source: @presstv/193085).
U.S. President Trump said he would call Netanyahu to advise against retaliating against Iran (Source: @pkpoi/142513).
Trump indicated that the United States has not yet agreed to a direct attack on Iran (Source: @SharghDaily/164463).
Trump warned that a failed deal could lead to a “commando raid” on Iran (Source: @lelotsenzura/92670).
U.S. officials emphasized that further escalation would only delay negotiations (Source: @naya_foriraq/77490).

key_developments: Israel’s air force intercepted all eleven missiles launched from Iran, confirming a full defensive success (Source: @VahidOnline/75999).
Two Iranian ballistic missiles aimed at northern Israel were shot down by Israeli air defenses (Source: @manniefabian/58479).
Iran employed solid‑fuel Chaybah ballistic missiles with a 1,450 km range from western bases targeting Ramat David (Source: @N12chat/211457).
Iran’s Civil Aviation Authority halted all flights at Imam Khomeini Airport until further notice (Source: @VahidOnline/76008).
Hezbollah fired rockets toward Israeli border communities, prompting alerts in the north (Source: @yediotnews25/130752).
Israeli minister Itamar Ben‑Gvir demanded that Tehran be set ablaze following the missile attacks (Source: @VahidOnline/76007).
U.S. President Trump said he would call Netanyahu to advise against retaliation against Iran (Source: @pkpoi/142513).
sections.threat_assessment: Iran continues to demonstrate long‑range missile capabilities, employing solid‑fuel Chaybah rockets capable of striking deep into Israeli territory. Hezbollah’s rocket fire from Lebanon adds a secondary front, increasing pressure on Israel’s northern defenses. Israeli interception successes show robust air defense, but the volume of attacks and political rhetoric suggest a heightened risk of broader escalation.
sections.regional_response: The United States is urging restraint, with President Trump signaling a willingness to intervene diplomatically and warning against immediate Israeli retaliation. Iranian officials warn of severe consequences for any Israeli action in Lebanon or against Iran, while Lebanese factions remain on high alert. Regional actors, including Saudi and Iraqi officials, are engaged in diplomatic contacts to manage the fallout.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian launch sites in western Iran is critical, given the reported range and payload of Chaybah missiles. Continued surveillance of Hezbollah rocket deployments along the Lebanon‑Israel border is required to anticipate further attacks. Flight suspensions at Imam Khomeini Airport may affect civilian movement and logistics, indicating broader security measures within Iran.
